mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% increase in housing units sold within 3 months signals a strengthening demand for durable goods, including equities, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with triggered HOUST signal are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in industrial production within 2 months signals a strengthening supply chain, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with triggered INDPRO signal are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the unemployment rate within 1 month signals a strengthening labor market, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with triggered UNRATE signal are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in real disposable personal income within 2 months signals a strengthening demand for consumer goods, including equities, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with triggered RSAFS signal are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% increase in payroll employment within 1 month signals a strengthening labor market, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with triggered PAYEMS signal are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the federal funds rate within 1 month signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with triggered FEDFUNDS signal are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in real GDP within 2 months signals a strengthening demand for durable goods, including equities, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with triggered GDPC1 signal are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
